subcategories.name: Coin Price Targets
subcategories.definition: Markets on whether a major, liquid cryptocurrency (BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP/Ripple, BNB, DOGE) reaches, exceeds, or holds a price level or % move by a deadline; settled automatically from exchange/oracle price feeds.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Continuous, publicly observable underlying that is highly recurrent and tightly anchorable to spot price and implied vol, so it is well-calibrated with minimal FLB.

subcategories.name: Memecoin Price Markets
subcategories.definition: Price/performance markets on low-cap, sentiment-driven memecoins (the long tail beyond liquid majors); same price-feed resolution but on assets with no fundamental anchor.
subcategories.flb_rationale: No fundamental anchor plus extreme volatility makes outcomes far less learnable and longshot-dominated, so it is expected to show the strongest favorite-longshot overpricing in crypto.

subcategories.name: Listings, Launches & Pre-Market (FDV)
subcategories.definition: Event and valuation markets on discrete crypto launch outcomes: exchange listings, token generation events, and pre-market or fully-diluted-valuation (FDV) levels at launch, rather than ongoing spot price.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolves on one-off listing/launch events and launch valuations (often announcement- or discretion-driven), not a recurrent observable price, so it is far less anchorable and learnable than spot-price markets.

subcategories.name: Team-Sport Game Markets
subcategories.definition: Head-to-head moneyline/spread/total markets on individual team-sport games and cup/playoff ties (soccer, basketball, football, hockey, baseball, cricket, rugby) across pro and college leagues.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Two-team games on dense, recurring schedules with deep liquidity and point-spread anchors — the canonical, well-calibrated FLB baseline (Sports slope ~1.0).

subcategories.name: Individual Head-to-Head (combat & racquet)
subcategories.definition: One-athlete-vs-one-athlete match markets — tennis, boxing, MMA/UFC, chess, and pickleball.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Outcomes ride on a single athlete's form/health with lumpier, less-frequent matchups and casual 'puncher's-chance' money on underdogs, driving stronger longshot overpricing than mean-reverting team rosters.

subcategories.name: Field / Outright Winner (motorsport & golf)
subcategories.definition: 'Win the event/field' markets among many simultaneous competitors — golf (PGA) and motor racing (F1/Grand Prix).
subcategories.flb_rationale: Dozens of entrants create many extreme longshots against one big favorite — the high-cardinality structure where longshot overbetting (the strongest FLB) is mechanically largest.

subcategories.name: Olympics & Rare Multi-Sport Events
subcategories.definition: Infrequent global multi-sport competitions — Summer/Winter Olympics and Winter Games markets.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Every-few-years, novel fields with sparse priors and heavy national/casual money mean low learnability and more erratic, typically stronger FLB than dense league play.

subcategories.name: Props, Drafts & Fantasy (non-game outcomes)
subcategories.definition: Markets not resolved by a game winner — player/stat props, draft order/selections, and fantasy outcomes.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolution is on player stats or draft order rather than a scoreline, with weaker price anchors and thinner priors, giving different calibration than game moneylines.

subcategories.name: MOBA (Team Strategy)
subcategories.definition: Match and tournament outcomes for 5v5 multiplayer-online-battle-arena titles played in structured, mostly franchised league circuits (Worlds/MSI, The International, etc.), resolved by best-of-3/best-of-5 series result.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Long Bo3/Bo5 series and stable franchised rosters give low per-match variance and high learnability, so favorites are reliably priced and longshot overpricing is muted.

subcategories.name: Tactical / FPS Shooters
subcategories.definition: Match outcomes for round- and map-based first-person/tactical shooters (defuse, objective, and respawn modes) in Major/circuit play, resolved by best-of map series.
subcategories.flb_rationale: High round/map-level variance and upset-prone Bo1/Bo3 maps make outcomes noisier and harder to anchor, producing stronger longshot overpricing than MOBAs.

subcategories.name: Daily Temperature
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a single calendar day's measured temperature - the daily high or low at a specific city's weather station - via an objective next-day meteorological reading (typically bucketed/threshold outcomes around a forecastable value). The many city tags and the high/low/temperature type tags are all instances of this one mechanism, not separate FLB buckets.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Daily recurrence + tight forecast/climatology anchoring + automated objective resolution make these the most learnable markets on the platform, so FLB should be minimal and uniform across cities and high-vs-low.

subcategories.name: Climate & Science
subcategories.definition: Long-horizon climate and earth-science propositions - annual or seasonal temperature records, climate milestones, and science events - rather than a single day's station reading.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Long horizon, low recurrence, and weaker anchoring make these far less learnable than daily-temperature markets, so expect stronger / qualitatively different FLB.

subcategories.name: Elections
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on who wins a scheduled election, primary, or party nomination (and seat/margin counts) by official vote tally on a fixed date — US and international races, federal to mayoral.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Vote-tally resolution on a fixed date with dense polling/market priors and recurrence across cycles and geographies — the most anchorable, best-calibrated political markets, so the weakest FLB.

subcategories.name: Legislation & Government Action
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on official government/institutional action — bill passage, votes, government shutdowns, budget/debt actions, and control-of-chamber procedural outcomes.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolve by procedural government action with negotiated/open timing and deadline brinkmanship — weaker priors than elections and longshot probability spikes near shutdown/passage deadlines.

subcategories.name: Appointments & Confirmations
subcategories.definition: Markets on who is nominated, appointed, or confirmed to a government position (cabinet, agency heads, picks) — a discrete personnel decision over a field of candidates.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Winner-take-all over a large insider-/rumor-driven candidate field (often negRisk multi-outcome) — thin public priors and many low-probability longshots drive strong FLB.

subcategories.name: Scandals, Legal & Political Events
subcategories.definition: Markets on one-off news/legal events around political figures — indictments, resignations, pardons, court outcomes, and document revelations (e.g., Epstein files).
subcategories.flb_rationale: Idiosyncratic one-shot events with no recurrence and thin priors — hard to anchor, the classic strong-FLB longshot bucket.

subcategories.name: Approval & Polling Metrics
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a continuous published political metric crossing a numeric threshold by a date — approval ratings and polling averages.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolve by a mean-reverting published metric crossing a numeric threshold (the political analog of a crypto price target) — continuous and highly anchorable, a mechanism distinct from one-shot event resolution.

subcategories.name: Awards & Voted Outcomes
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on the winner of an awards show or televised competition decided by an academy/jury/audience vote on a scheduled date, chosen from a fixed slate of nominees or contestants (e.g., Best Picture, Album of the Year, reality-show season winner).
subcategories.flb_rationale: Discrete winner-take-all among a known field decided by a voting body; pundit consensus makes frontrunners salient and dark-horse nominees the longshots - classic annually-recurrent, learnable awards-market FLB.

subcategories.name: Box Office & Streaming Charts
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a quantitative film/TV/music performance metric crossing a threshold or topping a chart, sourced from a public data provider (opening-weekend or total gross, Netflix Top 10 rank, Spotify chart position/streams).
subcategories.flb_rationale: Continuous metric thresholds anchored to professionally-tracked, short-horizon data (tracking, pre-sales, early chart movement) - highly forecastable and efficiently priced, so weaker/different FLB than the vote- or event-based siblings.

subcategories.name: Creator & Internet Metrics
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a social-media creator's milestone or platform metric - subscriber/view/follower counts, video performance, or internet-culture milestones (e.g., MrBeast subscriber targets, YouTube view records).
subcategories.flb_rationale: Same threshold mechanism as box office but long-horizon with viral, fat-tailed dynamics and round-number 'milestone' framing - longshot targets are systematically overbet, giving a distinctly lower-learnability, stronger-FLB profile.

subcategories.name: Celebrity Events
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on idiosyncratic real-world events about public figures - relationships, statements, appearances, scandals, or legal/health news (e.g., breakups, Joe Rogan podcast guests or announcements).
subcategories.flb_rationale: One-off, headline-driven events with low recurrence and weak anchoring (like political scandals) - hard-to-price tail outcomes draw heavy longshot speculation, distinct from metric- and vote-resolved siblings.

subcategories.name: Equity Price Markets
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on whether an individual company's stock price reaches, exceeds, or stays within a level/threshold (or range) by a date — a continuous price compared to a fixed strike, resolved off the closing/settlement price.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Single-name stocks carry idiosyncratic jump/news risk and concentrated retail/meme attention, fattening the longshot (deep-OTM strike) tail — expect the steepest FLB in Finance.

subcategories.name: Index & Commodity Price Markets
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on the level/threshold of a broad equity index or a commodity (e.g., S&P 500, gold, oil) by a date — the same price-vs-strike mechanism as equities but on deep, institutionally-arbitraged macro underlyings.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Macro benchmarks are among the most efficient, deeply-hedged underlyings, so longshot strikes price tighter — expect materially flatter FLB than single-name equities (efficiency/learnability split, like sports-by-league).

subcategories.name: Up/Down Directional Markets
subcategories.definition: Short-horizon binary 'will X be up or down / above-below the open' directional markets, typically daily/intraday, resolved by a price oracle (Pyth) rather than a chosen strike.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Symmetric ~50/50 props with no real longshot tail and maximal recurrence/anchorability (oracle-resolved, hyper-frequent) — near-efficient, serving as the near-flat FLB benchmark within Finance.

subcategories.name: Earnings & Corporate Events
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a discrete corporate event — quarterly earnings beat/miss, EPS/revenue/guidance, plus M&A, bankruptcy, leadership changes, and IPOs — rather than a continuous price path.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Event-driven and low-recurrence (quarterly/one-off), anchored to analyst consensus rather than a watchable price, giving a discrete-surprise resolution with different learnability than continuous-price markets.

subcategories.name: Earnings Call Mentions
subcategories.definition: Markets on whether, or how many times, a specific word/phrase is said by executives during a company's quarterly earnings call (e.g., 'Will the CEO say "AI" on the call?'). Resolved by call transcript/audio.
subcategories.flb_rationale: High-recurrence venue (quarterly x many firms) with deep per-CEO transcript history and a financial-specialist base -> learnable base rates, so longshot will-say-X props price tighter than one-off political events.

subcategories.name: Political Speech Mentions (SOTU)
subcategories.definition: Markets on whether a specific word/phrase is said during a State of the Union or comparable major political address (e.g., 'Will the President say "inflation"?'). Resolved by speech transcript/audio.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Once-a-year, single-speaker event with mass political public money and thin per-event recurrence -> noisier, politics-style longshot mispricing versus the quarterly specialist-driven earnings venue.

subcategories.name: Military Strikes & Kinetic Conflict
subcategories.definition: Discrete kinetic military events resolved by whether a specific attack/strike/incursion occurs by a deadline (e.g., 'Will Israel strike Iran by date X?', 'Will the U.S. attack Iran?', strikes on/by Hezbollah). Binary did-it-happen-by-deadline kinetic propositions.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Low-base-rate, headline/escalation-driven kinetic events on hard deadlines — least learnable, so the 'strike happens' longshots are systematically overpriced (classic FLB).

subcategories.name: War, Ceasefire & Diplomatic Resolution
subcategories.definition: Outcomes of active wars resolved by negotiated de-escalation — ceasefires, peace deals, war-ends-by-date, hostage/prisoner exchanges (Russia-Ukraine, Gaza). Resolves on multi-party diplomacy, not a single kinetic act.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Negotiations with repeatedly-slipping deadlines persistently overprice 'ceasefire/ends by date X' longshots — a de-escalation mechanism distinct from sudden kinetic strikes.

subcategories.name: Trade War, Tariffs & Sanctions
subcategories.definition: Economic statecraft resolved by policy announcements — tariffs, trade deals, sanctions, export controls (e.g., 'Will the U.S. impose tariffs on China/Mexico by date?'). Resolves on telegraphed, often-scheduled policy actions.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Telegraphed policy with a scheduled negotiation cadence is the most anchorable/learnable here, so prices are better-calibrated than kinetic-conflict longshots.

subcategories.name: Regime & Leadership Stability
subcategories.definition: Survival or fall of a foreign leader/regime over long horizons (e.g., 'Will Maduro leave office by date?', regime-change props). Status-quo-dominated leadership-tenure outcomes.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Strong long-duration status-quo base rate makes 'regime falls' a perpetual longshot that keeps overpricing — status-quo tenure dynamics with no deadline-kinetic or negotiation cadence.

subcategories.name: Product & Model Releases
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on whether a company ships, announces, or releases a specific product, model, or feature by a date deadline — Apple keynote/iPhone reveals, OpenAI and Grok model launches, Big Tech product/feature rollouts. Resolution = a discrete corporate announcement evaluated against a date threshold.
subcategories.flb_rationale: One-off, deadline-gated announcements with no recurrence; hype overprices aggressive early-ship 'longshot' dates, giving strong FLB and low learnability.

subcategories.name: AI Benchmarks & Platform Metrics
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on an observable measured quantity or ranking crossing a threshold — AI benchmark scores and leaderboard standings (e.g., the recurring monthly 'best AI model'), App Store #1, download/MAU counts. Resolution = a published metric or rank.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Quantified, frequently-updated leaderboards/metrics are anchorable and recurring, so they are more learnable and show a flatter FLB slope than one-off launches.

subcategories.name: Elon Musk & Social Platform Behavior
subcategories.definition: Markets on Elon Musk's personal public activity and X/Twitter platform actions — tweet/post counts, what he will say or do, follower counts, and X feature/policy changes.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Idiosyncratic individual behavior resolved by counting or parsing utterances — high-noise, unanchorable celebrity-longshot dynamics distinct from corporate release or metric markets.

subcategories.name: Macro Data Releases
subcategories.definition: Markets resolving on a scheduled, objectively-published statistical number that crosses a threshold on a known release calendar — inflation prints (CPI, PCE), growth/labor/activity data (GDP, jobs & unemployment, retail sales, PMI), and TSA passenger-throughput figures.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolves by an exogenous agency-published number with sharp published consensus forecasts and high regular recurrence — the most anchorable/learnable bucket, so longshots are just distribution tails of a well-forecast print.

subcategories.name: Monetary Policy (Fed)
subcategories.definition: Markets on Federal Reserve decisions and FOMC outcomes — rate cut/hold/hike at a given meeting, year-end or terminal target rate, number of cuts, and Fed leadership/Chair questions.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolves by a discretionary FOMC committee vote anchored extremely tightly by fed-funds futures (CME FedWatch), pricing favorites near-certainty — a different mechanism and a sharper anchor than a data print.

subcategories.name: Economic Policy
subcategories.definition: Markets on fiscal, legislative and executive economic actions — tariffs, government shutdowns, debt-ceiling outcomes, and budget/spending or stimulus legislation.
subcategories.flb_rationale: Resolves by political/legislative action with no consensus number or futures anchor, event-driven and irregularly recurring — weak anchorability behaves like Politics-Legislation FLB, not a forecastable print.
